title: Review iter-3: final convergence check
notes:
Confidence: 90% chance of no missed P0 bug. Least confident flags: (1) the downcast_ref doc issue (.3) — it's possible the Rust Any coercion works in some MSRV 1.80 contexts I did not manually compile-test; (2) the expand_vars modified=false branch (.5) — I traced the logic carefully but did not write a test to confirm the wasted alloc. Uncertainty driven by: not running a live reproducer for the require-ordering bug (I trust the code reading), and not verifying the downcast claim against rustc output.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — the code is clear and readable; the one idiom concern (mutual recursion for elsif) is a real structural smell but doesn't affect correctness today.
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one confirmed RFC compliance bug (require ordering) that a conformance test suite would catch immediately; one latent stack overflow with adversarial scripts that a fuzz test would catch; and one API doc that gives callers incorrect guidance on error downcasting.
Confidence: 95% no missed P0/P1. Least confident flags: none. Uncertainty driven by: rfc_vectors.rs integration tests not reviewed (40 tests, all pass). Would raise confidence if: RFC 5228 examples cross-checked directly.
Idiom rating: CLEAN — all P0/P1/P2 RUSTY issues resolved across 3 iterations.
Maintainer risk: LOW — P0 RFC compliance bug fixed, no remaining structural issues.
